business.headline: Investing in times of war: Panic, pause or buy the dip?Sensex and Nifty have seen declines as investors reacted to rising uncertainty, volatile crude oil prices and fears of a wider conflict. For many retail investors, this has meant seeing their mutual fund or stock market portfolios slip into the red.

Building businesses that last: 5 lessons for women entrepreneursAcross the country, millions of women are running businesses in their own way. Some operate small enterprises, others are building growing ventures, but each one contributes to livelihoods, job creation, and the strength of local economies.

Sensex down 600 points: Why is stock market falling today?The S&P BSE Sensex was down 566.31 points at 79,449.59, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 164.85 points to 24,601.05 as of 11:24 am. Sensex and Nifty shed over half a percent.

India asks refiners to maximise LPG output, sell only to IOC, BPCL and HPCLRefiners have been directed to increase LPG output and sell the fuel only to three state-run oil marketing companies, Indian Oil Corporation, Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited and Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Limited.

Gulf crisis disrupts LNG supply and your kitchen may feel the heatIf LNG supply disruptions persist, the impact may soon reach your kitchen, as higher global gas prices gradually filter into household gas bills and CNG costs across Indian cities.

Sensex, Nifty in red over Iran war: When will stock market bounce back?The ongoing West Asia conflict involving Iran, Israel and the US has raised fears that energy supplies could be disrupted. If oil supply tightens, crude prices may rise further.

Sensex, Nifty open lower as Iran war weighs in on investor sentiment; RIL up 2%The S&P BSE Sensex was down 368.75 points to 79,647.15, while the NSE Nifty50 lost 115.25 points to 24,650.65 as of 9:34 am.

Gulf nations may review foreign investments as Iran war strains financesThe review could affect a wide range of financial activities. These include investment pledges to foreign governments or companies, sports sponsorship agreements, contracts with businesses and investors, and even the possible sale of certain holdings.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market rise or fall today?Gift Nifty futures were trading at 24,649 at around 8:10 am, indicating that the benchmark Nifty 50 may open below Thursday's closing level of 24,765.9.

Rs 10,000 in PPF or SIP? Which builds more wealth in 10 yearsA simple annual investment of Rs 10,000 may not sound like much, but over time it can grow into a meaningful sum. The real question is where to invest it, i.e., the predictable returns of PPF or the market-linked potential of a SIP. So which one comes out ahead after ten years? Let's have a look!

No LPG or LNG shortage, India expands talks with global energy suppliersIndia currently imports about 195 million metric standard cubic metres per day (MMSCMD) of natural gas. Out of this, around 60 MMSCMD comes from Qatar. However, authorities said India is already exploring alternative markets to reduce dependence on any single supplier.

Sensex surges 900 points, Nifty tops 24,750; Reliance jumps 3%The Sensex ended the session 900 points higher, while the Nifty closed above 24,750, as investors stepped in to buy beaten-down stocks following the recent market correction.

Will Iran tensions shake Dubai's booming real estate market?Rising tensions involving Iran and parts of the Gulf are now testing that perception, with reports of attacks reaching areas of the UAE and raising questions about whether the region's instability could shake one of the world's hottest property markets.

Why do only 20% of women reach senior roles in the same company vs 49% men?Many organisations hire women in good numbers, yet far fewer make it to leadership positions within the same company. The gap raises an important question: what happens along the way that slows down women's career progression?

Adani Total Gas hikes industrial gas prices amid Middle East tensionsThe supply challenges come after attacks on Iran and retaliatory strikes by Tehran, which have disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. The narrow waterway between Iran and Oman is one of the world's most important energy routes.

Still think rural women aren't financially savvy? See how they manage moneyThe common belief is that rural women are not financially aware. Yet daily life in many households tells a different story. Women are budgeting carefully, building small savings and guiding family spending. Could rural women actually be the silent managers of household finances?

Tax deadlines coming up? Know the last dates for revised returns and advance taxTax deadlines tend to creep up quietly, and many taxpayers realise the dates only when time is running out. If you still need to correct your income tax return or plan to pay advance tax, it helps to know the key deadlines and what options are available.

India back on Russia's oil map as Iran war tightens supplyTwo oil tankers that were earlier expected to head towards East Asia have now switched their destination to India. Data from Kpler and Vortexa show the ships are carrying a combined 1.4 million barrels of Urals crude, a Russian oil grade.

Reliance shares gain 3%: Why is RIL rising today amid Iran warBrokerages said disruptions in the Middle East could tighten the supply of refined oil products globally. This could push refining margins higher, which directly benefits companies like Reliance that process crude oil into fuels such as diesel and petrol.

Rupee stabilises after record low, but oil price swings threaten outlookFor currency markets, the rupee's brief recovery may only be an interlude, as traders brace for more turbulence driven by oil prices and the shifting geopolitics of West Asia.

entertainment.headline: Allu Sirish has a funny wish after fiancee Nayanika pre-wedding ritual video Telugu actor Allu Sirish is set to marry Nayanika Reddy on March 6 in Hyderabad. The intimate ceremony will be attended by family, close friends and members of the Telugu film industry, following a series of traditional pre-wedding celebrations.

Janhvi Kapoor walks 3,500 steps to Tirumala shrine on her birthday. Watch Janhvi Kapoor marked her 29th birthday with a traditional pilgrimage to Tirumala. She reportedly fulfilled a personal vow and continued her spiritual practices with family and friends.

Govinda reacts to criticism over small shows and school event performances Bollywood actor Govinda has responded to social media criticism regarding his appearances at school events, emphasizing that his choices are not influenced by ego and highlighting the significance of the occasions he attended.

Revanth Reddy visits Rashmika-Vijay's Hyderabad home to bless newlyweds. Pics Telangana Chief Minister A Revanth Reddy personally visited the home of actors Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda in Hyderabad to extend his best wishes following their private wedding ceremony. The couple got married on February 26.

Dhurandhar: The Revenge trailer release date confirmed as mega rollout begins The makers of Dhurandhar: The Revenge have confirmed the trailer release date as the film's mega promotional rollout begins. With Ranveer Singh returning in dual roles, the spy-action sequel is gearing up for a massive theatrical release on March 19.

Allu Arjun marks 15 years with 'cutie' Sneha: Couldn't have done it without you Allu Arjun and Sneha Reddy are celebrating their 15th wedding anniversary, with the actor sharing a heartfelt note along with an adorable picture of the two on social media. The Allu family is also gearing up for another celebration, with Allu Sirish's wedding scheduled for March 6.

Kareena to Anupam, celebs react as India beats England in T20 World Cup semi-final India defeated England by seven runs in a thrilling semi-final to enter the T20 World Cup 2026 final. Bollywood celebrities like Kareena Kapoor, Anil Kapoor and Sonu Sood celebrated the big win on social media.

Inside Toxic vs Hai Jawani Toh clash: No Dhurandhar 2, but box office test continues Yash's Toxic may have avoided a clash with Dhurandhar 2, but its new release date could spell trouble for Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai. One strategic move has shifted things at the box office, and not every film may survive the impact.

Bridgerton's Nicola Coughlan recalls a drunk fan once commented on her body Nicola Coughlan, known for her role as Penelope in Bridgerton, has openly addressed the scrutiny over her body. She shared how she confronted online trolls and unwanted attention from fans through her work on the series.

Ben Affleck started an AI company, Netflix bought it: What it means for movies? Netflix has acquired InterPositive, an AI startup founded by Ben Affleck that automates film post-production tasks. This move aims to improve filmmaking efficiency while preserving human creativity and artistic control.

Alia-Ranbir to Varun: Bollywood shows up for Team India at World Cup semi-final A host of Bollywood celebrities were present at Mumbai's Wankhede Stadium during the India vs England T20 World Cup 2026 semi-final. India secured a place in the tournament's finals against New Zealand.

YouTuber Anurag Dobhal claims torture by family, says he is filming 'last video' YouTuber and Bigg Boss 17 fame Anurag Dobhal released a "last video" on YouTube claiming mental harassment by his family over his inter-caste marriage. He also detailed his struggles, including a suicide attempt.

The Boys 5 trailer: Homelander sets sights on immortality before clash with Butcher The trailer for the fifth and final season of The Boys is out. The video promises a high-stakes confrontation between Homelander and Billy Butcher, with the future of Supes and the world hanging in the balance.

Boong review: A BAFTA-winning gem that tells Manipur's story with a lot of heartRelease Date:Fri Mar 06, 2026 Boong re-releases in theatres on March 6 and this is a chance for cinephiles to give the film its due. The movie, which recently won the BAFTA Award for Best Children's & Family Film, is more than just a children's film. At a time when loud storytelling is often confused with meaningful filmmaking, Boong stands out for its warmth and sincerity.

Family drama stories will never go out of fashion in India: Rana Daggubati Actor-producer Rana Daggubati speaks about backing unconventional cinema, reflecting on the reception to Kaanta, and his global tequila venture Loca Loka.

Yami Gautam clarifies accidental like on reel mocking Kriti Sanon's award win Actor Yami Gautam has clarified her involvement after social media users noticed her 'liking' an Instagram reel seemingly critical of Kriti Sanon's Best Actress win at an award show. She claimed that her action was unintentional.

Oscars 2026: Priyanka Chopra joins Robert Downey Jr, Chris Evans as presenter Priyanka Chopra has joined the star-studded lineup of presenters for the 98th annual Academy Awards, to be held on March 15. Conan O'Brien will be the host of the prestigious ceremony.

Britney Spears' rep speaks after singer's DUI arrest: Needs to change her lifestyle Britney Spears' recent DUI (driving under the influence of alcohol and drugs) arrest in Ventura County, California, has prompted strong statements from her representative, who has expressed concern and called her actions 'completely inexcusable.'

Hrithik Roshan on fatherhood: No achievement competes with my love for my sons Hrithik Roshan shared an emotional post on fatherhood and the relevance of success and failure. He highlighted the importance of life over just winning.

general news.headline: Why Trump is wary of creating another China in IndiaHandcrafted ByOur Editors The US seems to have suddenly woken up to the 'mistakes' it made while dealing with China 20 years ago. It resulted in China being the economic powerhouse it is today. Now, Trump wants to avoid repeating those with India. But India never needed anyone for its rise.

Live: Balen Shah eyes landslide win, leads on 70 seats, KP Oli's party in single digitsNepal Election Results 2026 Live Updates: Vote counting is underway in Nepal's high-stakes general election in which rapper-turned-politician Balendra Shah's party gained leads in early counting. Out of a total of 275 seats in Nepal's lower house of parliament, counting is in progress for 165 seats that are elected by direct election. The remaining 110 seats will be filled later via a system of proportional representation. Voting took place on Thursday, months after a Gen Z-led protest movement forced Oli and his Communist Party of Nepal (Unified Marxist–Leninist) government out of power in September 2025. Stay tuned to indiatoday.in for the latest updates.

Karnataka to ban social media use for children under 16: CM SiddaramaiahThe Karnataka government plans to ban social media use for children under 16, amid rising concerns over smartphone addiction, online safety risks, and excessive screen exposure among young users. The announcement was made by Karnataka Chief Minister Siddaramaiah while he was presenting the 2026–27 state Budget on Friday.

'5 sixes to nahi khaunga': Axar Patel was ready to bowl the final over vs EnglandIndia vice-captain Axar Patel revealed he was ready to bowl the final over against England in the T20 World Cup semi-final. Shivam Dube ultimately bowled the last over to secure a thrilling seven-run victory for India, setting up a final clash with New Zealand.

Why Pakistan desecrated the grave of its only Nobel laureate, erased his memoryPakistan's only Nobel laureate, Abdus Salam, helped build the foundations of the country's nuclear programme. Because he belonged to the Ahmadiyya sect, his religious identity was stripped away, and his legacy was erased in Pakistan. Islamabad boasts of its nuclear weapons, but gives no credit to Salam.

Won't make China-like mistakes: US official says 'America First' in India trade dealUS Deputy Secretary of State Christopher Landau said Washington will prioritise its national interests in economic engagement with India and will not repeat the mistakes it believes it made with China two decades ago.

Live: Iran fires new wave of missiles, drones at Israel after strikes pound TehranUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: On Day 7, the war in the Middle East intensified as Iran launched fresh missile attacks on Israel and US bases, with Israel intercepting incoming strikes. Israeli forces also hit Hezbollah positions in Lebanon, where at least eight people were killed. The conflict has killed more than 1,230 people in Iran, over 70 in Lebanon and about a dozen in Israel, while six U.S. troops have died. The U.S. Embassy in Kuwait shut down after Iranian retaliatory strikes. The fighting has rattled global markets, pushed oil prices higher and slowed shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, while U.S. President Donald Trump signalled interest in influencing Iran's next leadership. Stay tuned to IndiaToday.in for live updates on the ongoing Middle East conflict.

Vijay, Trisha attend wedding together days after wife alleges affair with actorVijay and Trisha Krishnan attended the wedding reception of Kalpathi Suresh and Meenakshi Suresh's son. This is their first appearance amid the Jana Nayagan actor's divorce case.

India, this is the man you need to thank for discovering Jasprit BumrahT20 World Cup: Jasprit Bumrah bowled yet another riveting spell to take India through to the T20 World Cup final. India Today traced the man who discovered Bumrah, and turned him into the bowler he is today.

Rs 10,000 in PPF or SIP? Which builds more wealth in 10 yearsA simple annual investment of Rs 10,000 may not sound like much, but over time it can grow into a meaningful sum. The real question is where to invest it, i.e., the predictable returns of PPF or the market-linked potential of a SIP. So which one comes out ahead after ten years? Let's have a look!

Congress's 'gangs of Epstein' swipe after India gets US waiver on Russian oilAfter India received a 30-day waiver from the US to buy Russian oil currently stranded at sea as a result of the raging Middle East conflict, the Congress accused the government of letting the country's energy policy be dictated by Washington.

Nishant Kumar to be made JDU chief, Nitish Kumar to hold key party meet todayAccording to sources, many within the party believe Nishant Kumar's entry into active politics could help maintain unity and prevent potential splits at a time when the JD(U) faces a delicate political phase.

Dhoni's hilarious reaction to wife Sakshi's premature celebration goes viralT20 World Cup semi-final: MS Dhoni's hilarious reaction to wife Sakshi's premature celebration during the India vs England clash in Mumbai has gone viral. Dhoni was present at the Wankhede as India beat England to progress to the final of the T20 World Cup 2026.

How Robotic Knee Replacement is Redefining the Journey Back to MobilityEarly consultation with an orthopaedic specialist is essential. Delaying surgery in severe cases can lead to worsening deformity and reduced functional outcomes.
2 IAF pilots killed after Sukhoi jet crashes in AssamThe Sukhoi Su-30MKI fighter jet went missing on Thursday evening shortly after it took off from Jorhat and lost radar contact on Thursday evening.

India gets 30-day US waiver to buy Russian oil shipments stranded at seaThe United States has granted a temporary 30-day waiver allowing Russian oil cargoes stranded at sea to be sold to India, two senior U.S. officials told Reuters, aiming to ease pressure on global oil markets.

Boong review: A BAFTA-winning gem that tells Manipur's story with a lot of heartRelease Date:Fri Mar 06, 2026Boong re-releases in theatres on March 6 and this is a chance for cinephiles to give the film its due. The movie, which recently won the BAFTA Award for Best Children's & Family Film, is more than just a children's film. At a time when loud storytelling is often confused with meaningful filmmaking, Boong stands out for its warmth and sincerity.

Can't even pick New York mayor: Iran minister mocks Trump's demand on Tehran leaderIran's Deputy Foreign Minister Saeed Khatibzadeh described the ongoing Middle East conflict as an 'existential war' for Tehran, accusing the United States of being drawn into it by Israel due to what he called the 'delusion of a Greater Israel'.

Want to finish Iran first: Trump says Cuba next, 'just a question of time'Donald Trump says the US will focus on defeating Iran first but hinted Washington could soon shift its attention to Cuba, telling supporters it is "just a question of time."

Childhood obesity rising: Study finds 12.5%of teens in Kolkata schools are obeseA new study in Kolkata schools has found that one in eight adolescents aged 12–16 is obese. Experts say poor diet, high screen time and lack of physical activity are driving the rise in childhood obesity.

Inside Toxic vs Hai Jawani Toh clash: No Dhurandhar 2, but box office test continuesYash's Toxic may have avoided a clash with Dhurandhar 2, but its new release date could spell trouble for Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai. One strategic move has shifted things at the box office, and not every film may survive the impact.

MIT scientists create injectable mini livers that could reduce transplant needMIT engineers have developed injectable "satellite livers" made from liver cells and hydrogel microspheres. In animal tests, these mini tissue grafts survived for weeks and produced essential proteins. The innovation could eventually offer an alternative or temporary support for patients waiting for liver transplants.

Mohammad Amir is a Dhongi Baba: Sidhu slams Pak pacer after India reaches finalT20 World Cup: Navjot Singh Sidhu slammed Mohammed Amir after India reached the T20 World Cup final despite the Pakistan pacer predicting otherwise. Sidhu called Amir a "Dhongi Baba" as India once again proved his forecasts wrong.

Not using headphones on a flight? This airline may not let you flyThe change was introduced in February under the airline's "refusal to transport" section, which lists circumstances under which passengers can be denied travel or removed from a plane.

sports.headline: Should England continue with McCullum as head coach? Harry Brook shares his thoughts T20 World Cup 2026: England captain Harry Brook has given Brendan McCullum a "125%" vote of confidence despite a T20 World Cup semi-final exit, backing his coach to stay on despite pressure from the Ashes defeat.

India bowlers need to learn from Jasprit Bumrah: Sunil Gavaskar warns ahead of final T20 World Cup: Indian cricket legend Sunil Gavaksar, speaking exclusively to India Today, emphasised how the rest of the India bowling unit need to learn and try to replicate the clinical brilliance of their pace spearhead Jasprit Bumrah.

India's unshakable team spirit stood tall in Wankhede's run fest T20 World Cup semi-final: On a testing night against England, India's collective team spirit eventually paved the way for their narrow seven-run victory in the semi-final of the T20 World Cup 2026. The team's ability to respond collectively when the pressure peaked stood out in the nerve-wracking run fest.

Sanju Samson was on a different planet: Sunil Gavaskar hails heroics vs England T20 World Cup: Sanju Samson's stunning knock against England left Sunil Gavaskar in awe, with the legend saying the India opener looked like he was batting on a different planet. His brilliance powered India into the T20 World Cup final.

Sanju Samson on burden of expectations: Will feel lighter after T20 World Cup final T20 World Cup: Is the burden of expectations off Sanju Samson's shoulders? The Indian wicketkeeper claims that he will feel a lot lighter after the T20 World Cup 2026 final against New Zealand in Ahmedabad. Samson was the star of the show for India with the bat as they beat England in Mumbai.

Dhoni's hilarious reaction to wife Sakshi's premature celebration goes viral T20 World Cup semi-final: MS Dhoni's hilarious reaction to wife Sakshi's premature celebration during the India vs England clash in Mumbai has gone viral. Dhoni was present at the Wankhede as India beat England to progress to the final of the T20 World Cup 2026.

India, this is the man you need to thank for discovering Jasprit Bumrah T20 World Cup: Jasprit Bumrah bowled yet another riveting spell to take India through to the T20 World Cup final. India Today traced the man who discovered Bumrah, and turned him into the bowler he is today.

Varun Chakravarthy needs to switch off, bring him straight to final: Irfan Pathan T20 World Cup: Irfan Pathan urged the Indian team management to allow Varun Chakravarthy to switch off before the T20 World Cup final against New Zealand in Ahmedabad. Varun went for 64 runs in his four overs against England in the semi-final in Mumbai.

Does McCullum want to continue as England coach? New Zealand tactician reacts T20 World Cup: England coach Brendon McCullum reiterated that he would like to continue in his role despite their exit from the T20 World Cup 2026. McCullum's position has been under the scanner ever since England's horrendous Ashes series in Australia.

Mohammad Amir is a Dhongi Baba: Sidhu slams Pak pacer after India reaches final T20 World Cup: Navjot Singh Sidhu slammed Mohammed Amir after India reached the T20 World Cup final despite the Pakistan pacer predicting otherwise. Sidhu called Amir a "Dhongi Baba" as India once again proved his forecasts wrong.

Bapu Axar and Bumrah: Two Gujaratis rewrite history at WankhedeT20 World Cup: India's 253 lit up the T20 World Cup semi-final. Axar Patel's gravity-defying catches and Jasprit Bumrah's death-over mastery crushed England's chase. It was yesterday once more.

At the top together: Samson backs Abhishek Sharma to fire in T20 World Cup finalT20 World Cup: India wicketkeeper Sanju Samson has thrown his support behind out-of-form opener Abhishek Sharma, saying the youngster could still make an impact in the T20 World Cup final against New Zealand.

Jasprit Bumrah wasn't travelling to Ahmedabad alone. He took Team India with himT20 World Cup semifinal: India's 253 seemed safe. Yet Jacob Bethell's stunning counter-attack revived England's hopes before Jasprit Bumrah's control at the death secured a narrow win and India's passage to the T20 World Cup final.

How many do we score? Suryakumar jokes after England nearly chase 254 in MumbaiIndia captain Suryakumar Yadav admits being very nervous during their narrow seven-run win against England in the T20 World Cup semi-final. England almost chased down 254 as they finished their innings on 246/7 in their 20 overs.

'5 sixes to nahi khaunga': Axar Patel was ready to bowl the final over vs EnglandIndia vice-captain Axar Patel revealed he was ready to bowl the final over against England in the T20 World Cup semi-final. Shivam Dube ultimately bowled the last over to secure a thrilling seven-run victory for India, setting up a final clash with New Zealand.

Thala for a reason? Fans hail MS Dhoni's presence as India beat England by 7 runsMS Dhoni's fans started the 'Thala for a reason' trend on social media after India defeated England by seven runs to secure a place in the T20 World Cup final in the presence of the former India captain.

Jasprit Bumrah deserved the Player of the Match award vs England: Sanju SamsonT20 World Cup, IND vs ENG: Sanju Samson said Jasprit Bumrah deserved the Player of the Match award after India beat England by seven runs on Thursday and booked their berth in the final against New Zealand.

Axar's safe hands helped Wankhede breathe, tilted semifinal in India's favourAxar Patel's acrobatic fielding helped India regain control in the T20 World Cup semi-final against England. He took the most important catch of the match to dismiss Will Jacks in the 14th over.

Sublime Sanju Samson, 253 just enough as India pip England to reach World Cup finalT20 World Cup semifinal: Sanju Samson's dazzling 89 powered India to 253, which proved just enough against England in a battle of batting powerhouses in Mumbai on Thursday. Jacob Bethell's stunning hundred kept England alive until the final over. India will now travel to Ahmedabad to take on New Zealand in the final on Sunday.

Jacob Bethell lights up Wankhede with 45-ball century in semifinal against IndiaT20 World Cup, IND vs ENG: Jacob Bethell scored a 45-ball century in the semifinal of the tournament against India on Thursday, March 5 at the iconic Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai. But his knock went in vain as England lost by seven runs to crash out of the tournament.

MS Dhoni, Rohit Sharma cheer India from stands in semi-final against EnglandT20 World Cup, IND vs ENG: Former captains MS Dhoni and Rohit Sharma cheered for the Men in Blue in the semifinal against Harry Brook's England on Thursday, March 5 at the Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai

Rejuvenated Sanju Samson misses 100, but makes Harry Brook pay for his mistakeSanju Samson continued his brilliant form with an 89-run knock in the T20 World Cup semi-final against England. His powerful innings helped India set a strong foundation after Abhishek Sharma against departed cheaply.

Melbourne mayhem awaits: What to expect from F1's 2026 season openerFormula 1's 2026 season begins amid controversy over new hybrid regulations and geopolitical tensions. Despite criticism from top drivers and war concerns, the Australian GP promises thrilling unpredictability.

Smriti Mandhana joins Team Barbie with special doll before International Women's DaySmriti Mandhana has been honoured by Barbie ahead of International Women's Day, becoming the first cricketer to have a Barbie doll. She joins a global list of trailblazing women, including tennis legend Serena Williams, recognising her exceptional contributions to Indian cricket.

Abhishek Sharma flops in semifinal as poor run of T20 World Cup form continuesT20 World Cup, IND vs ENG: Abhishek Sharma scored nine runs off seven balls in the semifinal against Harry Brook's England at the Wankhede Stadium before Will Jacks accounted for his wicket.

IND vs ENG: Suryakumar Yadav unfazed by dew factor at Wankhede as England bowl firstIND vs ENG, T20 World Cup 2026 semifinal: India captain Suryakumar Yadav remains confident despite losing the toss as England opt to bowl first in the T20 World Cup 2026 semifinal at Mumbai's Wankhede Stadium on Thursday.

ICC responds to Sammy's plea, sends private charter for West Indies to return homeT20 World Cup 2026: A charter flight has been arranged for the West Indies cricket team after they were stranded in Kolkata for five days due to airspace restrictions following US and Israeli strikes on Iran.

Lakshya Sen enters quarterfinals of All England ChampionshipsIndia's Lakshya Sen overcame the disappointment of squandering four match points in the second game to dominate the decider against Hong Kong's Ng Ka Long Angus and reach the quarterfinals of the All England Open 2026 on Thursday.

Ellyse Perry to return in pink-ball Test against India? Alyssa Healy revealsAUSW vs INDW: Alyssa Healy provided an update on Ellyse Perry's participation in Australia's pink-ball Test against India, which begins on March 6 at the WACA in Perth. Perry missed the ODIs due to a quad injury.

Afghanistan sack Rashid Khan as T20I captain, announce squads for Sri Lanka seriesAFG vs SL: Afghanistan have removed Rashid Khan as their T20I captain while announcing the squads for their upcoming multi-format series against Sri Lanka. Rashid was dropped following Afghanistan's failure to qualify for the Super 8 stage of the T20 World Cup.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
